name: Customer opens the quote email and views the public quote page
description: End-to-end customer touchpoint: a quote email lands in Gmail mobile, customer taps the CTA, and the public quote renders correctly with no auth wall.

persona.name: Asha Patel
persona.context: Asked Diego for a fence repair quote yesterday. Sees the email on her phone over coffee.

preconditions: Diego sent a quote to Asha 5 minutes ago
Public quote URL active and unauthenticated-accessible

steps.action: Email is sent from Diego's name to asha@example.com
steps.expect: From: 'Diego Martínez via Paperwork Monsters'; subject mentions 'fence repair' and 'Diego'; preview text is meaningful
Renders correctly: brand color visible, no broken images, line items legible, CTA button tappable
Public quote page loads in browser; total, line items, business name, and Accept/Decline/Question buttons visible without scrolling on a 6.1" phone
Backend logs exactly one quote-open event with timestamp + user agent; Diego's /quotes page reflects it within ~10s

emails.subject: Your fence repair quote — Diego Martínez
emails.expect: Renders in: Gmail web/mobile, Apple Mail, Outlook web. Dark mode looks intentional, not inverted-and-broken

evaluationCriteria.check: Email feels like a personal message, not a SaaS notification
From-name uses contractor's name, not 'no-reply'; reply-to goes somewhere a human can read
Public link is signed/unguessable but doesn't require login; opening on a fresh device works
Email body has alt text on logo; CTA is a real link, not an image-only button
